column1: What AWS Step Functions state type enables executing several different execution paths simultaneously?
column2: Parallel state - useful for activities that don't depend on each other and can execute in parallel to make workflows complete faster

column1: When do messages move to an SQS dead-letter queue?
column2: When they meet the Maximum Receives parameter (number of times a message can be received) and have not been deleted

column1: What is the maximum message size for Amazon SQS?
How do you send messages larger than 256 KB using SQS?
column2: Save the file in S3 and send a link to the file through SQS

column1: What is the maximum message size for Amazon SNS?
What is the read capacity limit for a single Kinesis shard?
column2: 2 MB per second or 5 transactions per second

column1: Is there a limit on the number of consumers for a Kinesis stream?
column2: No limit on consumers as long as they stay within the stream's capacity based on number of shards

column1: What AWS service is best for ingesting large amounts of data in real time and performing real-time analytics?
What is the minimum latency for Kinesis Data Firehose analytics?
column2: At least 60 seconds (not suitable for real-time analytics)

column1: How are records distributed across Kinesis shards when using a partition key?
column2: Records with the same partition key will not be distributed across multiple shards (they stay together)

column1: What is the fan-out pattern in microservices architecture?
column2: Combining SNS (pub-sub) with SQS (queuing) to provide scalability, decoupling, and flexibility for message distribution to multiple services

column1: What type of SQS queue ensures messages are processed exactly once and in order?
Does message visibility timeout in SQS provide exactly-once delivery guarantee?
column2: No, visibility timeout prevents re-delivery during processing but doesn't guarantee exactly-once delivery like FIFO queues do

column1: Does SQS Long Polling address duplicate message processing?
column2: No, Long Polling optimizes message retrieval but does not address duplicate processing

column1: What is the difference between standard SQS and FIFO SQS queues?
column2: Standard queues provide at-least-once delivery with best-effort ordering; FIFO queues provide exactly-once processing and maintain strict message order
